_comment: Clinical decision pathway for Shingles aligned to NHS Pharmacy First criteria with strict eye-risk and timing logic.
label: Shingles (Herpes Zoster)

questions.text: When did the rash first appear?
questions.clinicalNote: Assessment is highest priority in the first 72 hours and still clinically relevant up to 7 days

questions.text: Is the rash only on ONE side of your body?
questions.clinicalNote: Unilateral rash is characteristic of shingles

questions.text: Do you have pain, burning, or tingling in the same area as the rash?
Is the rash near or around your eye?
Do you have any ear pain, hearing problems, or a rash inside your ear?
questions.clinicalNote: Ramsay Hunt syndrome — shingles affecting the ear

questions.text: Are you immunocompromised? (e.g. HIV, cancer treatment, on immunosuppressants)

emergencyOverrides.description: Combined eye-area and ear symptoms with shingles — highest acuity
emergencyOverrides.message: Eye symptoms together with ear pain or hearing changes during shingles can be an emergency. Call 999 or go to A&E now.

redFlags.description: Ophthalmic zoster risk due to rash near eye
redFlags.message: Shingles symptoms near the eye need urgent same-day assessment.

redFlags.description: Ramsay Hunt syndrome — shingles affecting the ear/facial nerve
redFlags.message: Ear pain and hearing changes with shingles (Ramsay Hunt syndrome) requires same-day urgent care.

redFlags.description: Immunocompromised patient — higher risk of complications, possible IV antiviral needed
redFlags.message: Shingles in immunocompromised patients needs urgent same-day assessment.

redFlags.description: Shingles in pregnancy — requires obstetric and infectious disease review
redFlags.message: Shingles during pregnancy requires same-day assessment. Please go to your maternity unit or urgent care.

eligibilityRules.reason: Rash onset beyond 7 days is outside pharmacy treatment window
Immunocompromised — requires GP or specialist management

eligibilityRules.condition: q2 === true && (q1 === 'Today (within 24 hours)' || q1 === '1–2 days ago' || q1 === '3 days ago' || q1 === '4–7 days ago') && q5 === false && q6 === false && q7 === false && q8 === false
eligibilityRules.reason: Uncomplicated shingles in treatment window without high-risk features

outcomeRules.reason: Shingles almost always causes a one-sided rash. A doctor should check other possible causes of your symptoms.

outcomeRules.condition: pharmacyEligible === false && q1 !== 'More than 7 days ago'

outcomeRules.reason: Outside treatment window — GP to assess symptom control and complications
Uncomplicated shingles within 72 hours — urgent pharmacy antiviral treatment

pharmacyTreatmentOptions: Aciclovir 800mg five times daily for 7 days
Valaciclovir 1g three times daily for 7 days (preferred where available)
safetyNetAdvice: Return immediately if: eye pain or visual changes develop, ear symptoms worsen, or you feel very unwell. Postherpetic neuralgia (nerve pain after rash resolves) is common — GP follow-up advised.
pathwayPatientDisclaimer: This pathway encodes timing and risk features for herpes zoster; antiviral eligibility, ophthalmic zoster, and Ramsay Hunt features require timely professional assessment — do not delay eye or ear symptoms.
